headlineSummary: The day’s discussion is mostly neutral-to-mixed: OpenAI has limited evidence for a mildly negative effect on writing norms [E3], while Anthropic draws both strong praise for Claude Code and sharp criticism of its policy stance [E1][E2].

ranking.summary: OpenAI’s signal is neutral overall but rests on limited evidence, with one comment saying ChatGPT made the writer worry their style could look AI-generated [E3].
Anthropic’s signal is mixed-neutral, with one comment strongly recommending Claude Code for security audits and another criticizing its policy position as morally troubling [E1][E2].

evidence.excerpt: I recommend that anyone who is responsible for maintaining the security of an open-source software project that they maintain ask Claude Code to do a security audit of it.

annotations.rationale: The commenter gives a broad recommendation to use Claude Code for security audits, signaling very strong confidence in its usefulness.

evidence.excerpt: Now Anthropic wants to have two narrow exceptions, on pragmatic and not moral grounds. To do so, they have to couch it in language clarifying that they would love to support war, actually, except for these two narrow exceptions.

annotations.rationale: The commenter condemns Anthropic's policy stance as effectively pro-war aside from narrow exceptions, which is a strong moral criticism.

evidence.excerpt: As somebody who used em-dashes a lot pre-ChatGPT, I have genuinely struggled with feeling I should change my writing style to appear more human.

annotations.rationale: The commenter links ChatGPT to a negative social effect: anxiety that their own writing style will be seen as AI-generated, reflecting mild negative sentiment toward the product's impact.
